items.title: AI Agent Identity and Permission Challenges: How Uber and Auth0 Are Rethinking Access Control
items.summaryShort: AI Agent Identity and Permission Challenges: How Uber and Auth0 Are Rethinking Access Control. Uber recently described an internal architecture for propagating identity across multi-agent AI workflows.

items.title: Presentation: From Hype to Strong Foundations: What the Rise, Fall and Resurgence of Agents Can Teach Us About Outlasting the Cycle
items.summaryShort: Presentation: From Hype to Strong Foundations: What the Rise, Fall and Resurgence of Agents Can Teach Us About Outlasting the Cycle. Aditya Kumarakrishnan explains how to move past the "amnesia phase" of AI.

items.title: GitHub Copilot Desktop App Targets Parallel Agentic Workflows
items.summaryShort: GitHub Copilot Desktop App Targets Parallel Agentic Workflows. GitHub has introduced the GitHub Copilot app, a desktop control centre for agent-native development that aims to keep engineers in charge while AI agents handle more coding work

items.title: Presentation: Automating the Web With MCP: Infra That Doesn’t Break
items.summaryShort: Presentation: Automating the Web With MCP: Infra That Doesn’t Break. Paul Klein discusses the distributed systems challenges of scaling cloud-hosted browser infra for AI agents.

items.title: Coinbase Postmortem Reveals How a Localized AWS Failure Triggered a Multi-Hour Trading Outage

items.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
items.summaryShort: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs. Here's what you need to do to get those pesky "write with Gemini" pop-ups to go away.

items.title: AI is hurting Apple in more ways than one: it may force iPhone price increases
items.summaryShort: AI is hurting Apple in more ways than one: it may force iPhone price increases. CEO Tim Cook said in a recent interview that the situation is "unsustainable."

items.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won't be selling AI
items.summaryShort: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won't be selling AI. Chi-Hua Chien has spent more than two decades as a venture capitalist, but he thinks like a cultural anthropologist.
